domain: Mauritanian guedra trance drumming circle (Tuareg/Mauritania; participants sit in A/B/A/B alternating circle; A's clap on beat 1, B's clap on beat 2; center dancers entrain to phase-coherent collective rhythm; trance emerges from sustained synchronization)
specific_mechanism: Two interleaved sub-groups alternating clap phases produce a constant-rate phase-coherent rhythm that entrains dancers; ABABAB ordering halves the per-group beat rate while doubling the aggregate.
llm_application: GUEDRA-INTERLEAVE: a distributed-training synchronization schedule. Split N workers into two interleaved sub-groups A and B (|A|=|B|=N/2). At each global step k, sub-group A pushes gradients at phase 0 (even sub-step) and sub-group B pushes at phase π (odd sub-step). The parameter server receives gradients at CONSTANT rate (alternating A → B → A → B), flattening straggler-induced bursts. Combined with bounded staleness Δ ≤ 1 sub-step. Differs from synchronous SGD (all workers push together) and asynchronous SGD (workers push independently) by explicit 2-PHASE INTERLEAVE that flattens server arrival rate.
novelty_claim: Synchronous AllReduce is phase-0-only. Asynchronous SGD is arbitrary-phase. Local SGD does periodic averaging. Decentralized SGD avoids server. Pipeline parallel uses micro-batches in pipeline. GUEDRA-INTERLEAVE differs by (a) TWO-PHASE WORKER SUBGROUP, (b) ALTERNATING-PHASE EXPLICIT, (c) BOUNDED 1-SUB-STEP STALENESS, (d) CONSTANT-RATE server stream. Combination of explicit 2-phase + bounded staleness is novel.
